input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. Also attached is an image showing seven CAD views (each boxed in black) of our vehicle design. The top, big view shows a close-up view of the design. The six smaller views on the bottom of the image show different complete views of the CAD of the vehicle and are provided for context. Note that the close-up view orientation matches one of the six complete view orientations. The close-up view may also have some components hidden (with respect to the corresponding complete view) for visualization of specific components. Looking at the close-up view, is/are any part of the the motor visible in the close-up view? Answer simply with yes or no.

retrieved_context:
RULE: v.2.2
            TITLE: visibility

            CONTENT:
            rule V.2.2: Visibility
V.2.2 Visibility 
a. The driver must have sufficient visibility to the front and sides of the vehicle 
b. When seated in a normal driving position, the driver must have a minimum field of vision 
of 100° to both sides 
c. If mirrors are required for this rule, they must remain in place and adjusted to enable the 
required visibility throughout all dynamic events.

RULE: t.5.3.1
            TITLE: the rotating part of the motor(s) ev.4.1 must be contained in a structural casing.

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.3.1: The rotating part of the Motor(s) EV.4.1 must be contained in a structural casing.
T.5.3.1 The rotating part of the Motor(s) EV.4.1 must be contained in a structural casing. 
The motor casing may be the original motor casing, a team built motor casing or the original 
casing with additional material added to achieve the minimum required thickness.  
• Minimum thickness for aluminum alloy 6061-T6:  3.0 mm  
If lower grade aluminum alloy is used, then the material must be thicker to provide an 
equivalent strength.   
• Minimum thickness for steel:  2.0 mm

RULE: t.1.8.3
            TITLE: positioning

            CONTENT:
            rule T.1.8.3: Positioning
T.1.8.3 Positioning  
The Firewall must extend sufficiently far upwards and/or rearwards and/or sideways where 
any point on the drivers body less than 100 mm above the bottom of the helmet of the tallest 
driver must not be in direct line of sight with any part given in T.1.8.1 above

RULE: t.5.3.2
            TITLE: a scatter shield must be included around the motor(s) when one or both:

            CONTENT:
            rule T.5.3.2: A Scatter Shield must be included around the Motor(s) when one or both:
T.5.3.2 A Scatter Shield must be included around the Motor(s) when one or both: 
• The motor casing rotates around the stator 
• The motor case is perforated

RULE: t.4.2.5
            TITLE: if an implausibility occurs between the values of the appss and persists for more than 100

            CONTENT:
            rule T.4.2.5: If an Implausibility occurs between the values of the APPSs and persists for more than 100
T.4.2.5 If an Implausibility occurs between the values of the APPSs and persists for more than 100 
msec, the power to the (IC) Electronic Throttle / (EV) Motor(s) must be immediately stopped 
completely.
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 65 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
(EV only) It is not necessary to Open the Shutdown Circuit, the motor controller(s) stopping 
the power to the Motor(s) is sufficient.
